LAW AND MOTION 302, VERIFIED APPLICATION OF MATTHEW J. BALL TO APPEAR AS COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE FOR THE DEFENDANTS DONALD BASILE, CARLOS NEVAREZ AND BIZZBLIZZ, INC. IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS' APPLICATION SATISFIES RULE 9.40 OF THE CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT. MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: SUZANNE BOLANOS; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 =302/SB

REPLY MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER TO VERIFIED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 57886779) FILED BY DEFENDANT BASILE, DONALD IN HIS INDIVIDUAL CAPACITY NEVAREZ, CARLOS "ART" BIZZBLIZZ, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS DONALD BASILE, CARLOS NEVAREZ AND BIZZBLIZZ, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED, WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD IS SUSTAINED, WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, TO PROVIDE PLAINTIFF WITH AN OPPORTUNITY TO ALLEGE FACTS WHICH MEET THE SPECIFICITY REQUIREMENT FOR PLEADING FRAUD. IN CALIFORNIA, "FRAUD MUST BE PLED SPECIFICALLY; GENERAL AND CONCLUSORY ALLEGATIONS DO NOT SUFFICE." (LAZAR V. SUPERIOR COURT (1996) 12 CAL.4TH 631, 645.) AS NOTED BY DEFENDANTS, THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT ALLEGE SPECIFICALLY HOW, WHEN, WHERE, TO WHOM, AND BY WHAT MEANS THE REPRESENTATIONS WERE TENDERED. (LAZAR ,12 CAL.4TH 631, 645.) FURTHERMORE, THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT ALLEGE FACTS STATING HOW PLAINTIFF WAS DAMAGED BY THE ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATIONS OR WHY PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO RELIEF. THE FACT THAT PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ADEQUATELY PLED ITS FRAUD CLAIM AGAINST DEFENDANTS MEANS THAT ITS SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR JOINT AND SEVERAL LIABILITY OF MANAGEMENT PRINCIPALS OR MATERIALLY AIDING PERSONNEL IS ALSO SUSTAINED, WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM UNDER CALIFORNIA CORPORATIONS CODE SECTIONS 25401 AND 25401.1 IS PARTLY DEPENDENT ON IT PLEADING FACTS WHICH ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANTS MADE FRAUDULENT MISREPRESENTATIONS. ADDITIONALLY, IT IS UNCLEAR FROM THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT THAT THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS BROUGHT PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CORPORATIONS CODE SECTIONS 25401 AND 255401.1. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489; DEPT. 302. =302/EHG

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. HAROLD R. JONES, ESQ. of Severson & Werson APC, Tel No. 415-398-3344 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JAMES W. MORANDO, ESQ. of Farella Braun & Martel LLP, Tel No. 415-954-4400 appearing for Defendants Donald Basile, Carlos Nevarez, and Bizzblizz, Inc.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court vacates its tentative ruling, and orders as follows: DEFENDANTS DONALD BASILE, CARLOS NEVAREZ, AND BIZZBLIZZ, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff needs to allege clearly between the corporate defendant and the individual defendants. Plaintiff must state what the individual defendants said or what they had a duty to say or not say that caused the alleged fraud, or how the individual defendants allegedly materially aided. Defense counsel shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature which may be e-mailed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-01-2015 Continued To Feb-17-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-19-2015 Continued To Dec-16-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-02-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS DONALD BASILE, CARLOS NEVAREZ AND BIZZBLIZZ, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Demurrer sustained, with ten days leave to amend. Defendants' demurrer to the first cause of action for fraud is sustained, with ten days leave to amend, to provide Plaintiff with an opportunity to allege facts which meet the specificity requirement for pleading fraud. In California, "fraud must be pled specifically; general and conclusory allegations do not suffice." (Lazar v. Superior Court (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) As noted by Defendants, the complaint does not allege specifically how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered. (Lazar ,12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) Furthermore, the complaint does not allege facts stating how Plaintiff was damaged by the alleged misrepresentations or why Plaintiff is entitled to relief. The fact that Plaintiff has not adequately pled its fraud claim against Defendants means that its second cause of action for Joint and Several Liability of Management Principals or Materially Aiding Personnel is also sustained, with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff's claim under California Corporations Code sections 25401 and 25401.1 is partly dependent on it pleading facts which allege that Defendants made fraudulent misrepresentations. Additionally, it is unclear from the face of the complaint that the second cause of action is brought pursuant to California Corporations Code sections 25401 and 255401.1. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

Off Calendar Jun-19-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Harold R. Jones, of Severson & Werson, APC., Tel. 415.398.3344, for plaintiffs;
James W. Morando, of Farella Braun & Martel LLP, Tel. 415.954.4400, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS DONALD BASILE, CARLOS NEVAREZ, AND BIZZBLIZZ, INC.'S Motion To Stay Or, In The Alternative, Dismiss Action Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure Section 410.30 is denied. The subject matter of the instant action and Utah action are different and there is little chance of inconsistent rulings. The parties are ordered to coordinate discovery when possible. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
